items.title: Seedance 2.0: Advancing Video Generation for World Complexity
items.summary: Researchers introduce Seedance 2.0, a significant advancement in video generation capabilities, enabling the creation of more complex and realistic virtual worlds.

items.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
items.summary: Factory, an AI coding platform, has achieved a $1.5 billion valuation, signaling strong investor confidence in AI-powered development tools for large organizations.

items.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
items.summary: Google introduces an 'AI Mode' in Chrome, offering users novel ways to interact with and explore web content through artificial intelligence.

items.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
items.summary: Luma has unveiled a new AI-powered production studio, launching with the 'Wonder Project' specifically tailored for faith-based content creation.

items.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
items.summary: Upscale AI is reportedly in discussions to secure new funding that would value the company at $2 billion, highlighting rapid growth in the AI sector.

items.title: Codex for (almost) everything
items.summary: OpenAI announces an expansion of its Codex model's capabilities, aiming to apply its code generation and understanding across a broader range of tasks and domains.

items.title: Introducing GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
items.summary: OpenAI unveils GPT-Rosalind, a specialized AI model designed to accelerate and enhance research within the life sciences sector.

items.title: GameWorld: Towards Standardized and Verifiable Evaluation of Multimodal Game Agents
items.summary: Researchers propose GameWorld, a new framework for standardized and verifiable evaluation of multimodal AI agents operating within game environments.

items.title: RationalRewards: Reasoning Rewards Scale Visual Generation Both Training and Test Time
items.summary: A new research paper introduces RationalRewards, a method that uses reasoning-based rewards to improve the scalability of visual generation models during both training and testing.

items.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
items.summary: A VentureBeat article highlights a free alternative called Goose that offers similar AI coding capabilities to the paid Claude Code, potentially disrupting the market for developer tools.

items.title: Exploration and Exploitation Errors Are Measurable for Language Model Agents
items.summary: New research demonstrates that exploration and exploitation errors, critical aspects of decision-making, can be quantitatively measured in language model agents.

items.title: SciFi: A Safe, Lightweight, User-Friendly, and Fully Autonomous Agentic AI Workflow for Scientific Applications
items.summary: SciFi introduces a new autonomous, agentic AI workflow designed to be safe, lightweight, and user-friendly for various scientific applications.

items.title: Numerical Instability and Chaos: Quantifying the Unpredictability of Large Language Models
items.summary: This paper explores the inherent numerical instability and chaotic behavior in large language models, providing a framework to quantify their unpredictability.

items.title: The creator of Claude Code just revealed his workflow, and developers are losing their minds
items.summary: The workflow of Claude Code's creator has been revealed, generating significant excitement and discussion among developers keen to optimize their AI-assisted coding practices.

items.title: Join the OpenClaw Challenge: $1,200 Prize Pool!
items.summary: Developers are invited to participate in the OpenClaw Challenge, an initiative with a $1,200 prize pool aimed at fostering innovation in AI agent development.
